info.name: @ubyjerome/meta-manager
info.description:
Complete REST API collection for the meta-manager package.

Collection variables (edit in Postman under the collection or in an Environment):
  baseUrl  = http://localhost:3000
  entity   = books  (the route prefix you mounted the entity on)
  recordId = (paste a UUID after creating a record)
  parentId = (paste a parent UUID for child-relation requests)

request.description:
Create a new entity record.

The response includes an auto-generated `uuid` and `slug` (derived from `title_name`).

Required and optional fields depend on the `createSchema` Joi config passed when the entity was initialised. If no schema is configured all fields are accepted.

Reserved fields auto-populated by the package — do not send these:
  uuid, slug, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at

body.raw:
{
  "title_name": "Things Fall Apart",
  "description": "A classic novel by Chinua Achebe",
  "owned_by": "user-uuid-here",
  "added_by": "user-uuid-here",
  "created_by": "user-uuid-here",
  "meta_key": "featured_book",
  "meta_value": "true",
  "extra_data": [],
  "meta_data": []
}

response.name: 201 Created
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": true,
  "status": "Created",
  "statusCode": 201,
  "data": {
    "uuid": "b336ce54-7d69-47fb-93ef-ce1b03d13add",
    "title_name": "Things Fall Apart",
    "slug": "things-fall-apart",
    "description": "A classic novel by Chinua Achebe",
    "status": "active",
    "extra_data": [],
    "meta_data": [],
    "deleted_at": null,
    "created_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z"
  },
  "message": "books created"
}

response.name: 400 Validation Error
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Bad Request",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "data": [],
  "message": "\"title_name\" is required",
  "errorMessage": "\"title_name\" is required"
}

response.name: 409 Duplicate
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Conflict",
  "statusCode": 409,
  "data": [],
  "message": "Duplicate entry",
  "errorMessage": "E11000 duplicate key error collection: books index: slug_1"
}

request.description:
Insert multiple records in one request.

Accepts either:
  - A top-level JSON array: [{...}, {...}]
  - An object with an items key: { "items": [{...}, {...}] }

Validation runs on every item before any insert. If one item fails, the entire request is rejected. The error message identifies the failing index: Item 1: "title_name" is required

response.name: 201 Created
400 Item Validation Error
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Bad Request",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "data": [],
  "message": "Item 1: \"title_name\" is required",
  "errorMessage": "Item 1: \"title_name\" is required"
}

query.description: Filter by any field using filter[fieldName]=value

request.description:
Retrieve all records with full pagination, sorting, field projection, and arbitrary field filtering.

Pagination params:
  page   - page number (default: 1)
  limit  - records per page (default: 20, hard max: 500)
  sort   - any document field name
  order  - asc or desc

Field projection:
  fields=uuid,title_name,status  (comma-separated)

Filtering:
  filter[fieldName]=value  — multiple filters are ANDed

Soft-deleted records are always excluded.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Fetch a single record by its uuid or MongoDB _id.

Accepts an optional ?fields= projection to return only specific fields.

Returns 404 if the record does not exist or has been soft-deleted.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": true,
  "status": "OK",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "uuid": "b336ce54-7d69-47fb-93ef-ce1b03d13add",
    "title_name": "Things Fall Apart",
    "slug": "things-fall-apart",
    "description": "A classic novel by Chinua Achebe",
    "status": "active",
    "extra_data": [],
    "meta_data": [],
    "deleted_at": null,
    "created_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z"
  },
  "message": "books retrieved"
}

response.name: 404 Not Found
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Not Found",
  "statusCode": 404,
  "data": [],
  "message": "books not found",
  "errorMessage": "notFound"
}

query.description: Filter by event type: create, update, delete, restore

request.description:
Retrieve audit history for a specific record.

Requires auditLog to be enabled on the entity.

Query params:
  limit - records per page (default: 20)
  page  - page number (default: 1)
  event - optional filter: create, update, delete, restore

Returns paginated audit records with the document's change history.

response.name: 200 OK
400 Audit Not Enabled
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Bad Request",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "data": [],
  "message": "Audit log is not enabled for books"
}

query.description: true = all, or comma-separated aliases e.g. chapters,reviews
Page size for the 'chapters' child set
Sort field for the 'chapters' child set
Sort direction for the 'chapters' child set

request.description:
Fetch a record and embed its registered child entities as paginated sub-collections.

includeChildren:
  true              - embed all registered children
  chapters,reviews  - embed specific aliases only

childDepth:
  1 (default) - immediate children only
  2           - children and their children (grandchildren)

Per-child pagination (replace 'chapters' with the actual alias):
  childPage[chapters]=2
  childLimit[chapters]=5
  childSort[chapters]=created_at
  childOrder[chapters]=asc

Each child key in the response is a full paginated result object.

response.name: 200 OK with children
404 Not Found
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Not Found",
  "statusCode": 404,
  "data": [],
  "message": "books not found",
  "errorMessage": "notFound"
}

request.description:
Find all records where :field equals :value.

Change status/active in the path to any field/value pair:
  /by/owned_by/user-uuid-here
  /by/meta_key/featured_book
  /by/libraryId/lib-uuid-here

Returns a full paginated result. All standard pagination and sort params apply.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Search records by a query string.

q is required — returns 400 if absent.

searchFields overrides the entity's searchableFields config for this request.

If no searchFields are provided and none are configured on the entity, MongoDB full-text search ($text) is used — requires a text index.

Search is case-insensitive regex when field names are provided.

All standard pagination params apply.

response.name: 200 OK
400 Missing q
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Bad Request",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "data": [],
  "message": "Search query param 'q' is required",
  "errorMessage": "Search query param 'q' is required"
}

request.description:
Return the total count of non-deleted records, with optional filtering.

Supports the same filter[field]=value params as GET /all.
Soft-deleted records are excluded.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Check whether a record exists where :field equals :value.

Useful for uniqueness checks before create:
  /exists/slug/my-slug
  /exists/isbn/978-0-435-90553-6

Always returns 200. Never 404. Data is { exists: true } or { exists: false }.

response.name: 200 Exists
200 Not Exists

request.description:
Update a record identified by uuid or _id.

Only fields included in the request body are changed — fields not sent retain their current values.

If title_name is changed, slug is automatically regenerated.

Validation runs against the entity's updateSchema (all fields optional by default).

Lifecycle events fired:
  update          - once, with the full diff
  update.field    - once per each changed field key

response.name: 200 Updated
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": true,
  "status": "OK",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "uuid": "b336ce54-7d69-47fb-93ef-ce1b03d13add",
    "title_name": "Things Fall Apart (Revised Edition)",
    "slug": "things-fall-apart-revised-edition",
    "description": "A classic novel by Chinua Achebe",
    "status": "active",
    "extra_data": [],
    "meta_data": [],
    "deleted_at": null,
    "created_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z"
  },
  "message": "books updated"
}

response.name: 400 Validation Error
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Bad Request",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "data": [],
  "message": "\"status\" must be one of [active, inactive, archived]"
}

response.name: 404 Not Found
response.body:

request.description: Partial update — identical to PUT at the data layer. Only fields in the body are changed.

request.description:
Update a single named field. The field name is in the URL path.

Request body must be: { "value": <newValue> }

Supports dot-notation for nested fields:
  /field/status                     top-level field
  /field/extra_data.0.tokenName     first element of extra_data array
  /field/meta_data.0.price          nested object field

This is the recommended way to update a specific nested key without sending the entire document.

response.name: 200 Updated
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": true,
  "status": "OK",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "uuid": "b336ce54-7d69-47fb-93ef-ce1b03d13add",
    "title_name": "Things Fall Apart",
    "slug": "things-fall-apart",
    "description": "A classic novel by Chinua Achebe",
    "status": "active",
    "extra_data": [],
    "meta_data": [],
    "deleted_at": null,
    "created_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z"
  },
  "message": "books field \"status\" updated"
}

response.name: 400 Missing value
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Bad Request",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "data": [],
  "message": "Request body must contain { value }"
}

response.name: 404 Not Found
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Not Found",
  "statusCode": 404,
  "data": [],
  "message": "books not found",
  "errorMessage": "notFound"
}

request.description:
Example of dot-notation targeting the tokenName property inside the first element of extra_data.

This fires:
  update.extra_data             (field-level event)
  update.extra_data[*]          (wildcard array event)
  update.extra_data[tokenName].Zugacoin V2  (named element event, if subscribed)

response.name: 200 Updated

request.description:
Soft-delete a record by setting deleted_at to now and status to archived.

Soft-deleted records are invisible to all read endpoints (GET /all, GET /:id, search, findBy, count) but remain in the collection.

Use POST /:id/restore to reverse this.

The delete lifecycle event fires with the document's final state as whatWas.

response.name: 200 Deleted
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": true,
  "status": "OK",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": null,
  "message": "books deleted"
}

response.name: 404 Not Found
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Not Found",
  "statusCode": 404,
  "data": [],
  "message": "books not found",
  "errorMessage": "notFound"
}

query.description: Permanently removes the document. Cannot be undone.

request.description:
Permanently removes the document from the MongoDB collection. Cannot be undone.

Requires ?hard=true. Without it the request performs a soft delete.

response.name: 200 Hard Deleted
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": true,
  "status": "OK",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": null,
  "message": "books deleted"
}

response.name: 404 Not Found
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Not Found",
  "statusCode": 404,
  "data": [],
  "message": "books not found",
  "errorMessage": "notFound"
}

request.description:
Restore a soft-deleted record.

Sets deleted_at back to null and status back to active.
The record becomes visible to all read endpoints again.

The restore lifecycle event fires with the restored document state.

response.name: 200 Restored
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": true,
  "status": "OK",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "uuid": "b336ce54-7d69-47fb-93ef-ce1b03d13add",
    "title_name": "Things Fall Apart",
    "slug": "things-fall-apart",
    "description": "A classic novel by Chinua Achebe",
    "status": "active",
    "extra_data": [],
    "meta_data": [],
    "deleted_at": null,
    "created_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-27T10:00:00.000Z"
  },
  "message": "books restored"
}

response.name: 404 Not Found
response.body:
{
  "isSuccess": false,
  "status": "Not Found",
  "statusCode": 404,
  "data": [],
  "message": "books not found",
  "errorMessage": "notFound"
}
